title: BOSS: incident triage
prompt: Live incident. Everything you've learned, one report. In auth.log: (1) find the attacking IP — the source with the most Failed password lines; (2) write that IP into report.txt; (3) the attacker dropped a calling card: a hidden directory holds a note file NAMED after their IP — read it and submit the flag. Pipeline, fields, tallies, dotfiles: the full analyst kit.

details.restate: Three deliverables: identify the top attacking IP from auth.log, put that IP in report.txt, and find the hidden note named after the IP — it holds the flag.

io.call: grep 'Failed password' auth.log | awk '{print $13}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -n
io.returns: (tallies per IP, top attacker at the bottom)
io.because: the top-talker pipeline from three missions ago — now under fire
the calling card is hidden the same way .workdir was

help.plain: It's sec-01 + sec-03 wearing a trench coat: top-talker pipeline, then a dotfile hunt named after the answer.
help.analogy: The final exam where every question is one you've already answered — just all at once, with sirens on.
help.mnemonic: tally → report → hidden note named after the IP.
help.nudge: Which IP wins the failed-password tally? That exact string is both what goes in report.txt and the NAME of a hidden file.
